title: lupinus argenteus subsp. argenteus
intro: Lupinus argenteus subsp. argenteus, also known as silvery lupine, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the genus Lupinus and is native to the western United States and Canada. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of silvery lupine include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antioxidant, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Silvery lupine can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, and its leaves and flowers can also be used in tinctures or dried for later use.
